# Key Quora Ads Statistics for 2026 - SHNOco

## Summary
Comprehensive compilation of Quora Ads performance statistics and B2B case studies for 2026. Quora reaches 400M+ monthly active users arriving through search (high-intent research mode). The most cited platform case studies: Webflow (-83% CPA, +152% signups), monday.com (20% post-click CVR), Atlassian (92% lower CPC).

## Key quotations / statistics
- "Businesses using Quora Ads report conversion rates up to 4 times higher than on other advertising platforms"
- "60% of Quora's advertising revenue comes from B2B campaigns, with B2B ads on the platform yielding 4x more conversions than equivalent campaigns on other platforms"
- **B2B case studies:**
  - Webflow: 83% reduction in CPA, 152% increase in signups via Promoted Answers + interest targeting + auto-bidding
  - Atlassian: 92% lower CPC, 30% more engaged leads vs other platforms (Topic + Keyword Targeting)
  - monday.com: 20% post-click conversion rate, higher ROI than all other channels tested
  - Amazon Global Selling: 29.4M content views, CTR 2x B2B category average (India market)
  - DuckDuckGo: 9.34% CTR on Promoted Answers, 17.5M impressions, CEO-authored content
- **Cost benchmarks:**
  - Minimum CPC: $0.01; minimum CPM: $0.20 (one of lowest floor costs)
  - "Quora CPMs remain stable at $8-15 for B2B technology audiences" vs LinkedIn $75-90 CPM
  - "Quora CPC is 65% lower than comparable Google search campaigns, 40-50% lower than Meta"
  - "Quora leads have approximately 28% higher lifetime value vs leads from some other platforms"
- "Quora earns approximately USD 20 million annually in revenue" (small platform but high-intent)

## Annotations for stinger-forge
- **Primary source for `guides/08-quora-ads.md`** case studies section
- The Webflow and monday.com case studies are the strongest proof points for B2B SaaS viability - include both in `guides/08-quora-ads.md`
- The CPM comparison ($8-15 Quora vs $75-90 LinkedIn for similar B2B tech audiences) is a powerful channel-fit argument for budget-constrained early-stage companies
- The Command Brief open question "Is Quora still viable in 2026?" is partially answered: Yes, but as a supporting/complement channel not a primary one, with specific fit criteria (technical buyers, "vs" comparison queries, early research stage)
- The 20% post-click CVR from monday.com is an outlier worth noting but also requires the 2-month optimization context
